Walkthrough:Pokémon Platinum/Part 16

From Bulbapedia, the community-driven Pokémon encyclopedia.
< Walkthrough:Pokémon Platinum
Jump to navigationJump to search

Contents

  • 1 Mt. Coronet (south)
    • 1.1 1F
    • 1.2 2F
    • 1.3 3F
    • 1.4 Lower summit
    • 1.5 4F
    • 1.6 Upper summit
    • 1.7 Tunnel
    • 1.8 Summit
    • 1.9 5F
    • 1.10 6F
    • 1.11 7F

Mt. Coronet (south)

Make sure you brought Pokémon with the HMs Surf, Strength, Rock Smash, and Rock Climb.

1F

Items
Item Location Games
Dawn Stone Southeast corner (requires Surf)  Pt 
Protein On the northern edge of the large hill in the center (requires Surf)  Pt 

Enter this area of Mt. Coronet using the entrance from either Route 207 or Route 208. Pick up a few items that were not accessible before. Surf across the northernmost pool of water to the northeastern corner of the room. Climb up the rocks, and ascend the stairs.

2F

Available Pokémon (2F)
Pokémon Games Location Levels Rate
Clefairy
D P Pt
Cave

Golbat

Cave
39 10%
Graveler

Cave
38 20% 0% 20%
Graveler
D P Pt
Cave
36,38 0% 30% 0%
Nosepass
D P Pt
Cave
37 5%
Medicham
D P Pt
Cave
37,39 20% 20% 0%
Medicham
D P Pt
Cave
39 0% 0% 10%
Chingling
D P Pt
Cave
36,38 10%
Bronzong
D P Pt
Cave
37 20%
A colored background means that the Pokémon can be found in this location in the specified game. A white background with a colored letter means that the Pokémon cannot be found here.
Items (2F)
Item Location Games
Max Repel On ledge north of stairs from first floor  Pt 
Red Shard On small rock west (and slightly north) of Max Repel (hidden)  Pt 
Escape Rope In corner south-east of exit to Route 207 (requires Strength)  Pt 
TM80 (Rock Slide) North-east of exit to Route 207 (requires Strength)  Pt 
Max Revive South-west of exit to small northern cavern (requires Strength)  Pt 
Black Flute Given by Looker while trying to enter small northern cavern  Pt 
Star Piece On a rock at end of small cavern across from the stairs leading from 3F to 2F (hidden)  Pt 

From here on out, Mt. Coronet is an enormous maze. You may want to use Super Repels in some areas, but the wild Pokémon are high-leveled enough to be good for training against. It would be a good idea to keep the Dowsing Machine app on the Pokétch screen, as there will be a lot of hidden items.

Anyway, the exit in the southwestern corner of the room leads to a dead-end ledge on Route 207, where you can pick up a previously-unreachable Iron and Timer Ball.

Items
Item Location Games
Timer Ball South of the higher exit from Mount Coronet (requires Surf, Strength, and Rock Climb)  Pt 
Iron Ledge on the southeastern end of the route, after taking the higher exit from Mount Coronet (requires Surf, Strength, and Rock Climb)  Pt 

Back inside the mountain, don't forget to pick up TM80 (Rock Slide), as the move it contains is very useful.

Once you reach the northern exit, Looker will show up. Knowing that both Cyrus and the Galactic Commanders are out of his league to fight, he'll let you go on ahead since you're the only one powerful enough to take them on. He will give you the Black Flute before you go. After that, go through the exit and you'll be in 3F.

3F

Available Pokémon (3F)

A colored background means that the Pokémon can be found in this location in the specified game. A white background with a colored letter means that the Pokémon cannot be found here.
Items (3F)
Item Location Games
Max Repel Near the rock in north-west corner (hidden)  Pt 
Team Galactic
Trainer Pokémon
Galactic Grunt
Reward: $1720
Stunky♀ Lv.43
No item
Galactic Grunt
Reward: $1720
Murkrow♂ Lv.43
No item

When you arrive head to the set of stairs on the right to briefly return to 2F to find a hidden Star Piece. You'll also deal with your first two Galactic Grunts, who shouldn't be that much of a challenge if you use the right Pokémon. Afterwards, take the exit to the outdoors, and you'll find yourself in the summit area of Mt. Coronet!

Lower summit

Available Pokémon (summit)
Pokémon Games Location Levels Rate
Golbat
D P Pt
Grass

A colored background means that the Pokémon can be found in this location in the specified game. A white background with a colored letter means that the Pokémon cannot be found here.
Items (summit)
Item Location Games
Max Potion In the empty spot in middle of grass east of exit from 3F (hidden)  Pt 
Energy Root In an empty spot in the grass on the ledge above the eastern entrance to 4F (reached via Rock Climb) (hidden)  Pt 
Star Piece On boulder on ledge above eastern entrance to 4F (reached via Rock Climb) (hidden)  Pt 
Blue Shard On boulder behind breakable rock due south of western entrance to 4F (requires Rock Smash) (hidden)  Pt 
Revival Herb In the patch of grass south of the western entrance to 4F (hidden)  Pt 
Star Piece On west side of breakable rock due south of northern exit from 4F (requires Rock Smash) (hidden)  Pt 
Moon Stone On a boulder north of the entrance to the tunnel to the northern cavern (hidden)  Pt 
Revival Herb In the horizontal row of grass patches, northwest of the movable boulder (hidden)  Pt 
Full Restore On the narrow "empty" horizontal ledge, toward the left (hidden)  Pt 
Stardust On a boulder at the east end of the far northernmost ledge (hidden)  Pt 
Energy Root In the empty space in the corner of the grass patch west of entrance to 5F (hidden)  Pt 

You are now in the southern area of the summit. As it is constantly hailing here, non-Ice-types will receive damage in battle, though you can easily use Super Repels to prevent battles. Also, this area is full of hidden items. From where you are, head east and then north to the Rock Climb-able wall. Near the large patch of grass is the western entrance to 4F, and near another scalable wall is the eastern entrance. Climb that wall and you'll find a grassy area with some hidden items. The eastern 4F entrance serves little purpose at this point, although there are a couple of hidden items worth picking up.

Back outside in the summit, make your way to the northwestern entrance to 4F, in the large grass patch.

4F

Items (4F)
Item Location Games
Yellow Shard On a boulder directly west of eastern entrance, surrounded by breakable rocks (requires Rock Smash) (hidden)  Pt 
Heal Powder Behind the breakable rock north-west of eastern entrance, near the water (requires Rock Smash) (hidden)  Pt 
Stardust On a boulder on the ledge north of eastern entrance (hidden)  Pt 
Sun Stone On a boulder in the north-west corner of room, behind a breakable rock (requires Rock Smash) (hidden)  Pt 
Full Heal In the area above the entrance to the cave above the waterfall (hidden)  Pt 
Max Revive On the ledge east of the water area above the waterfall (hidden)  Pt 
Ultra Ball On the ledge north of exit to snowy summit (hidden)  Pt 
Team Galactic
Trainer Pokémon
Galactic Grunt
Reward: $1600
Houndour♂ Lv.40
No item
Golbat♂ Lv.40
No item
Houndour♂ Lv.40
No item
Galactic Grunt
Reward: $1600
Stunky♂ Lv.42
No item
Golbat♂ Lv.40
No item

Once inside from the west entrance, use Rock Climb to ascend to the upper area of 4F and head for the exit to the east, defeating two more Galactic grunts standing in your way.

Upper summit

Once through the exit from 4F, follow the path and take the stairs down and you will find a narrow chasm with two more Rock Climb walls and another cave entrance that will take you to a special tunnel.

Tunnel

Available Pokémon (Tunnel)
Pokémon Games Location Levels Rate

Items (Tunnel)
Item Location Games
Green Shard On a boulder on the southernmost ledge (hidden)  Pt 
Rare Candy In the middle of the highest and easternmost ledge (just south of the grunt) (hidden)  Pt 
Green Shard On a wall behind the breakable rock on the highest ledge west of the exit to northern cavern (requires Rock Smash) (hidden)  Pt 
Max Potion Several steps south of the exit to the northern caverns (hidden)  Pt 
Stardust On the corner boulder behind the breakable rocks directly southeast of the exit to the northern caverns (requires Rock Smash) (hidden)  Pt 
Heal Powder On the corner boulder next to a larger boulder behind a breakable rock southwest of the Stardust (requires Rock Smash) (hidden)  Pt 
Team Galactic
Trainer Pokémon
Galactic Grunt
Reward: $1720
Golbat♂ Lv.43
No item
Galactic Grunt
Reward: $1560
Murkrow♀ Lv.39
No item
Glameow♀ Lv.42
No item
Murkrow♀ Lv.39
No item
Galactic Grunt
Reward: $1680
Croagunk♂ Lv.38
No item
Croagunk♂ Lv.42
No item
Stunky♂ Lv.40
No item

You don't have to explore this part of the cave yet if you don't want to. You can battle three Galactic Grunts and find some hidden items in here. Also, a fourth grunt is guarding the entrance to a secret passage that leads to Mt. Coronet North Side's 1F. You can't go through here until you've stopped Team Galactic for good.

Summit

This time, ascend using the Rock Climb wall facing west and follow that path west. This will lead you to the entrance to 5F. Once again, there are some hidden items along the way.

5F

Items (5F)
Item Location Games
Max Elixir 3 steps west and one step south of the easternmost boulder (hidden)  Pt 

There isn't much here and the path is pretty straightforward, with only one hidden item. Take the nearby stairs to 6F.

Items (6F)
Item Location Games
Full Restore In the empty space behind the first Galactic Grunt (hidden)  Pt 
Rare Candy Northwest of the southeastern corner boulder (hidden)  Pt 
Team Galactic

Galactic Grunt

Golbat♂ Lv.41
No item

You can find two more hidden items here as well as two more Galactic Grunts. Take the stairs to 7F.

7F

Available Pokémon (7F)

Team Galactic
Trainer Pokémon
Galactic Grunt
Reward: $1640
Golbat♀ Lv.39
No item
Croagunk♀ Lv.40
No item
Murkrow♀ Lv.41
No item

All you need to do is defeat one last grunt and head for the exit where, at last, you have reached Spear Pillar! It's time to put an end to Cyrus's plans for the last time.
